subject: English / First Flight
chapter_name: Poems – How to Tell Wild Animals, The Ball Poem
overall_learning_objectives: Understand both poems in depth with emphasis on humour, exaggeration, loss, and responsibility
Analyze literary devices like imagery, repetition, and tone
Interpret themes and messages beyond the literal meaning
Develop critical thinking and discussion skills
Express understanding through writing, discussion, and visualization

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Poetic Overview
topics_covered: Authors: Carolyn Wells and John Berryman
Overview of both poems and their styles
Initial discussion on humour, observation, loss, and responsibility
teaching_strategy: Introduce poets and their writing styles
Ask students to share experiences of observing animals or dealing with small losses
student_activities: Discuss poem titles and predict themes and tone
Note initial thoughts on humour and reflection in poetry
assessment: Oral Q&A on poets and poem expectations
Check engagement in predictions and discussion

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Analysis – How to Tell Wild Animals
topics_covered: Observation as a theme and literary device
teaching_strategy: Line-by-line reading with discussion of meaning and humour
student_activities: Explain why these lines are funny or memorable
Summarize the poem in 4–5 sentences including theme and style
assessment: Oral responses to humour and style
Check written summaries for clarity and understanding

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Analysis – The Ball Poem
topics_covered: Read aloud and discuss the boy’s reaction
Theme of loss, responsibility, and emotional reflection
Imagery and repetition used to convey feelings
teaching_strategy: Explain each stanza and highlight key words
Discuss how the boy’s grief teaches a life lesson
student_activities: Identify words showing loss and reflection
Discuss why money cannot replace certain things
Summarize the poem in 4–5 sentences emphasizing theme and message
assessment: Oral Q&A on emotional depth and life lesson
Check written summaries for comprehension and insight

lesson_plan.title: Comparative Discussion – Themes and Devices
topics_covered: Compare humour in 'How to Tell Wild Animals' and reflection in 'The Ball Poem'
Identify literary devices: imagery, exaggeration, repetition, tone
Explore underlying messages in both poems
teaching_strategy: Create a comparative table on the board
Lead a guided discussion on humour vs reflection and learning from poems
student_activities: Fill in a chart comparing themes, tone, and literary devices
Discuss in pairs or small groups how the poems convey messages differently
assessment: Evaluate chart for completeness and accuracy

lesson_plan.title: Creative Reflection and Expression
teaching_strategy: Use drawings to reinforce comprehension and interpretation
student_activities: Write a short paragraph imagining yourself as a wild animal or the lost ball
Draw an illustration depicting humour, observation, or loss from the poems
assessment: Discuss lessons learned from both poems

teaching_aids: Printed or projected texts of both poems
Images of wild animals and playground balls
Charts for literary devices, themes, and tone
Flashcards with key vocabulary and examples of humour or reflection
teacher_tip: Encourage students to notice humour, exaggeration, and reflective tone. Promote discussion to interpret lessons from both poems. Connect poems to students’ own experiences for deeper understanding.
